seoTitle: How to Find Your Outlook Password Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Locate a forgotten Outlook password through Windows Credential Manager or your browser's saved logins, without a full account reset.
intro: A forgotten Outlook password doesn't always mean starting over with a full reset. If the credential was ever saved on your device, you can dig it up through Windows Credential Manager or your browser's autofill settings. This guide walks through both routes so you can get back into your inbox quickly.

steps.heading: Launch System Credential Manager
steps.body: Open the Windows search bar, type Credential Manager, and launch the utility from your system tools.

steps.heading: Filter for Microsoft Office logins
steps.body: Select the Windows Credentials category and scan the list for an entry tied to Outlook or Office.

steps.heading: Reveal the hidden password
steps.body: Expand the matching credential row, click the show link, and enter your local device PIN to unmask the text.

steps.heading: Check your browser as an alternative
steps.body: If you access Outlook through the web, open Chrome or Edge password settings, search for the Outlook or Live entry, and click the eye icon to read it.

steps.heading: Confirm the password still works
steps.body: Try logging in with the recovered password to confirm it's accurate and current before relying on it going forward.

faq.question: Where does Outlook actually store my saved password?
faq.answer: It's typically cached either in Windows Credential Manager for the desktop app or in your browser's password vault if you use Outlook on the web.

faq.question: What if I manage multiple Outlook accounts on one computer?
faq.answer: Each account will have its own separate credential entry, so look carefully through the list to find the one matching the specific domain you need.

faq.question: Do I need admin rights to view a saved password?
faq.answer: You typically just need your own device PIN or biometric confirmation, not full administrator access.

faq.question: What should I do if no credential is saved anywhere?
faq.answer: If nothing appears in either location, you'll need to use Outlook's standard account recovery process to reset your password instead.
